primary_annotation: Heterogeneous; dominant FKBP15-like + coiled-coil (CCDC39/ODF2) family; cytosolic
rationale: Giant mixed OG with weak, heterogeneous BLAST/Pfam support (<3% seqs annotated). Pfam hits (FKBP15, coiled-coils) indicate cytoskeletal / protein-interaction roles, not biomineralization. Non-secreted.

key_domains_or_hits: Hint module (85/91); Warthog 1 BLAST hits; 2 Trypsin hits
secretion_profile: Mostly TM (73) + SP+TM (3); a few GLOB
rationale: Hedgehog-type Hint-module proteins are membrane-anchored signalling/self-cleaving proteins. In calcifying metazoa Hedgehog signaling can influence skeletogenic cell fate, but evidence here points more broadly to morphogen/cell-fate signalling than direct matrix formation.
Pentapeptide-repeat proteins mimic B-DNA; typically cytosolic/defense. No link to Ca2+/CaCO3 biology.
Nuclear/mitochondrial DNA replication-fork helicases. Housekeeping; no mineralization role.
No assignable domains or homology. Worth retaining as lineage-specific candidate but cannot rate without further analysis (structure prediction, synteny).

primary_annotation: Mixed secreted/ECM-like (collagen VI / mussel adhesive plaque-like)
key_domains_or_hits: Collagen alpha-5(VI); Adhesive plaque matrix protein; tRNA-synt_2 (1)
rationale: Hits to collagen VI and byssal/adhesive-plaque matrix proteins plus enrichment for signal peptides suggests secreted ECM/adhesive components, which in calcifying systems can organise the mineralization matrix.

secretion_profile: Strongly secreted and membrane-anchored (SP+TM 11; SP 11; TM 4)
rationale: Predominantly SP+TM with PRIMA1 (proline-rich membrane anchor) hit: cell-surface, likely plasma-membrane anchored. Cell-surface anchors can tether matrix/ectoenzymes (plausible interface for mineral-seeding) but direct evidence is indirect.
Golgi/ER mannosyltransferases glycosylate secreted matrix glycoproteins. Indirect contribution possible via glycosylation of matrix proteins.
Pure ankyrin-repeat scaffolds; protein-protein interactions. No secretion or TM; no direct mineralization tie.

primary_annotation: Bacterial-like pentapeptide-repeat protein (same family as OG0001976/OG0022524)
rationale: Same family as OG0001976; likely intracellular.

secretion_profile: Mostly cytosolic (GLOB 22) with some SP (4) and TM (1)
rationale: Dark-matter OG; no homology. Small fraction secreted — candidate for further study.

Exostosin/GT47 = heparan-sulfate (animal) or xyloglucan (plant) polymer chain extension; Golgi type-II membrane enzymes. Heparan sulfate/sulfated polysaccharides are major components of biomineral organic matrices (e.g. skeletal/shell GAGs).

2OG dioxygenases include collagen prolyl/lysyl hydroxylases (relevant for ECM crosslinking) but top BLAST hits are plant secondary-metabolism oxidases (Kihadalactone/GA2ox), so matrix role is unlikely.

primary_annotation: Mixed / poorly supported; hints of serine aminopeptidase S33
rationale: Only 2 BLAST hits and 1 Pfam hit out of 24; annotations are heterogeneous and low-confidence.
Secreted nucleoside hydrolases act on nucleotide metabolism. Secretion is notable but nucleoside hydrolysis has no canonical role in CaCO3 deposition.

primary_annotation: Amidase-family enzyme (Glu-tRNA-Gln amidotransferase subunit A / AmiE-like)
rationale: Cytosolic/mitochondrial in their canonical roles; secreted amidases hydrolyse amides broadly. No direct mineralization link.
Large TM fraction suggests membrane proteins but functional assignment not supported.

primary_annotation: Mixed; weak hits to hexamerin and collagen IV
rationale: One collagen-IV hit in a TM-dominated family suggests a membrane-anchored ECM-like protein but support is weak.
Tetraspan membrane proteins involved in membrane microdomains/eisosomes; not a known mineralization factor.

Arylsulfatases hydrolyse sulfate esters on glycosaminoglycans/sulfated polysaccharides. Coccolith-associated polysaccharides and other biomineral matrices are heavily sulfated; ARSB orthologs are implicated in skeletal/shell matrix turnover.
Housekeeping translation-related enzyme.
Small-molecule/polysaccharide sulphotransferases can sulfate matrix GAGs and acidic polysaccharides; secretion further supports an extracellular role. Direct evidence modest (only 3 Pfam hits).
Stress-response / detoxification enzymes; no mineralization role.
Chloroplast/plastid chlorophyll catabolism; not related to calcification.
Cytosolic acyl-CoA-dependent acetyltransferases.
Nitric-oxide detoxification enzyme; no mineralization role.
Protein-protein scaffolds; typically cytosolic.
Integral membrane proteins of unknown function.

FG-GAP repeats are canonical integrin-alpha β-propeller motifs that mediate cell-matrix (and divalent-cation-dependent) adhesion. Calcifying tissues rely on integrin-matrix interactions; worth follow-up.

primary_annotation: NLR / LRR-containing NLRC3-like innate immune receptor
rationale: Innate-immune pattern-recognition receptors; no mineralization role.
Translation elongation factor; housekeeping.
Same family as OG0014250; secreted amidase without mineralization link.
No homology or Pfam support.

primary_annotation: HECT E3 ubiquitin ligase (HECD1 / UPL3) with ankyrin repeats
rationale: Ubiquitin ligases; protein-turnover; cytosolic.
Small-molecule oxygenase; secondary metabolism.
Amino-acid metabolism; not secreted.
Organellar translation/splicing factors.

primary_annotation: cAMP-dependent PKA regulatory subunit (+ one Apolipoprotein hit)
rationale: Canonical cytosolic PKA-R subunit; the signalP profile is inconsistent with that annotation — may reflect poorly predicted/fragmented ORFs.
DNA-repair helicase; housekeeping.
Dark-matter but strongly secreted — potential candidate for unknown secreted matrix factor.
Dark-matter.
PDZ scaffolds are cytosolic protein-interaction modules.
HS6ST sulfates heparan sulfate chains — directly relevant to acidic sulfated polysaccharide matrices that nucleate/regulate CaCO3 deposition in many calcifiers. Secreted/Golgi topology is consistent.
Dark-matter.
Dark-matter.
Dark-matter.

key_domains_or_hits: Trypsin (10); Trypsin_2 (3); snake-venom serine protease hits
rationale: All members carry signal peptides plus a trypsin domain — canonical secreted proteases. Extracellular proteases remodel biomineralization matrices (e.g. shell/bone matrix processing).
Reductases of sugar/carbonyl metabolism; cytosolic.
SCF ubiquitin-ligase substrate adapter; cytosolic.
Dark-matter.
PP2Cs include Ca2+/Mg2+-dependent Ser/Thr phosphatases involved in Ca2+ and stress signaling; indirect link to Ca2+ homeostasis possible.

primary_annotation: DegP-like serine endoprotease with PDZ domains
rationale: HtrA/DegP-family quality-control proteases; not known as matrix remodelers in calcifiers.
Function unknown but topology (SP+TM) is consistent with cell-surface/periplasmic proteins.
ER-anchored electron donor to P450s; general redox, no mineralization link.
Dark-matter.
Dark-matter with some membrane-secreted signal; candidate for further study.
Same family as OG0021517.
WD40 scaffolds; cytosolic.
Dark-matter.
Dark-matter.
Same family as OG0001976/OG0010867.
Dark-matter.
BMP-2-inducible kinase (BIKE/BMP2K) and cyclin-G-associated kinase participate in membrane trafficking; BMP-signaling link (bone morphogenetic protein pathway) is indirect but noteworthy.
ER-network dynamics; no mineralization role.
GT8 glycosyltransferases build pectin/polyuronate and glycosaminoglycan backbones. Acidic polysaccharide matrices underpin many calcified structures.
Dark-matter.
Chloroplast chlorophyll catabolism; not calcification related.
Glycosyltransferase-like domain acting on secreted glycans; possible indirect role in matrix glycan decoration.
Dark-matter.
Integral membrane proteins of unknown function.
Dark-matter.
Dark-matter with a strong secretion signal — candidate for lineage-specific secreted (matrix?) protein.
2-OG/Fe(II) JmjC hydroxylases primarily act on histones/splicing factors, but the Fe(II)/2-OG chemistry is the same used by collagen/matrix hydroxylases; direct matrix role is speculative.
